message:
fix(conformance,net/udp): prevent thread-local panic on drop and extend empty-buffer guard to all UDP receive paths

conformance/mod.rs:
- Replace `CURRENT_LAB_CONFORMANCE_SESSION.with(...)` with `try_with(...)` in
  the LabConformanceSessionGuard Drop impl. The `with()` accessor panics if the
  thread-local has already been destroyed (e.g., during thread shutdown). Using
  `try_with` + `let _ =` silently ignores that edge case, matching the pattern
  used elsewhere in the crate for thread-local cleanup guards.

net/udp.rs:
- Rename `empty_datagram_recv_from_buffer_error()` to
  `empty_udp_receive_buffer_error(op: &str)` and accept the calling method name
  so the error message identifies which UDP operation triggered it.
- Add empty-buffer early-return guards to `poll_recv` and `poll_peek_from`,
  matching the existing guard in `poll_recv_from`. Previously only `recv_from`
  rejected zero-length buffers; `recv` and `peek_from` would silently pass an
  empty slice to the kernel, producing platform-dependent behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
